text:
### explain handle grammar + path
`event:<id>` ¬∑ `llm:<id>` ¬∑ `session:<id>[@<ts>]` ¬∑ `hook:<id>[@<ts>]` ¬∑ `txn:<surface>:<id>` ¬∑ `sub:<channel>`. The store is daemon-owned, so the CLI forwards to the daemon `explain` RPC (`server.rs:284`) exactly like `who` (via `daemon::blocking::call`); the engine is a pure `&Store` query. `--redact` swaps prompt/slice/response bodies for `sha256:<hash> (<n> bytes)`; default prints full content. [...] Usage: tenex-edge explain [OPTIONS] <HANDLE>

Arguments:
  <HANDLE>  The artifact handle: `event:<id>`, `llm:<id>`, `session:<id>[@<ts>]`, `hook:<id>[@<ts>]`, `txn:<surface>:<id>`, or `sub:<channel>` [...]       --json    Emit the raw joined record as JSON instead of the human view
      --redact  Replace prompt/transcript/response bodies with `sha256:<hash> (<n> bytes)` [...] ### window_hash join thread
`distill_session` returns the verbatim `DistillCapture` ‚Üí runtime hashes the exact fed slice into `window_hash` and (i) records the `llm_calls` row with it, and (ii) passes the **same** `window_hash` into `drive(..., window_hash, |r| r.on_distill(...))`. `drive` writes it into the status receipt's `changed_summary` JSON (alongside `session_id`). So `explain event:<30315>` ‚Üí `receipts_by_artifact_ref(event_id)` ‚Üí read `changed_summary.window_hash` ‚Üí `llm_calls_by_window_hash` ‚Üí the exact LLM inputs. (Non-distill publishes carry no window_hash ‚Üí no LLM join, correctly.) [...] ### Test evidence that `explain event:<30315>` surfaces the LLM inputs
`explain::tests::explain_event_surfaces_the_llm_inputs` seeds an llm_call + its status receipt (joined by window_hash via the real `changed_summary_json`) into a temp in-memory store, then asserts the joined output carries the exact **system prompt** and **transcript slice** (plus model/raw_response). `cli::explain::tests::event_render_surfaces_the_llm_inputs` proves the human renderer. All 421 lib tests pass (was 419). [...]       commands: [{"kind":"refresh","key":"segments:2:6:status21:te-18bec9b2e639762b-0","reason":"refresh"}]
LLM inputs: (none ‚Äî this publish was not distill-driven)
``` [...] `event:<id>` ¬∑ `llm:<id>` ¬∑ `session:<id>[@<ts>]` ¬∑ `hook:<id>[@<ts>]` ¬∑ `txn:<surface>:<id>` ¬∑ `sub:<channel>`. The store is daemon-owned, so the CLI forwards to the daemon `explain` RPC (`server.rs:284`) exactly like `who` (via `daemon::blocking::call`); the engine is a pure `&Store` query. `--redact` swaps prompt/slice/response bodies for `sha256:<hash> (<n> bytes)`; default prints full content. [...] 2. **Persisted detail is a flattened summary, not the full interactive audit API.** The rich Trellis calls (`why_resource_command`, `dependency_path`, `why_changed`) run **in-process at commit time** ‚Äî that's how status attributes a publish to the `activity` input ‚Äî but what's *stored* for later `explain` is the summary + the LLM join, not a re-runnable dependency graph. And `changed_summary` currently lists node **ids** (`{"inputs":[2,3],"derived":[6]}`), not human labels ‚Äî a cheap enrichment left as follow-up. [...] - **Label the node ids.** `changed_summary` currently says `{"inputs":[2,3],"derived":[6]}` (`instrument.rs:95-121`) ‚Äî meaningless a week later, and meaningless to any agent. At each drive seam, flatten with the graph's `debug_name` map: `{"inputs":["activity","arm"],"derived":["status-content/te-9fc2"]}`. Receipt schema v2; old rows stay readable. [...] **F3 ‚Äî audit explanations are latest-per-key and ephemeral.** `record_transaction_audit` overwrites `audit.node_changes` / `resource_commands` / `output_frames` per key every commit and clears them when audit is Disabled (`audit.rs:52-146`, `audit_types.rs:16-24`). `why_changed`/`why_resource_command` answer *"why did this key LAST change,"* nothing historical. Historical "why" must come from persisted commits + replay, not a live query. This bounds `probe why` and the acid test.
